title: Constructors of an 'abstract' class should not be declared 'public'
description: Declaring a constructor as 'public' in an abstract class can be misleading, as abstract classes cannot be instantiated directly. Constructors in abstract classes should be 'protected' or 'package-private' to prevent direct instantiation and clarify the intended usage.
recommendation: Use 'protected' or package-private constructors in abstract classes to prevent direct instantiation.
impact: Code clarity and correct usage of abstract classes are improved, reducing the risk of misuse.

checks.message: Use 'protected' or package-private constructors in abstract classes to prevent direct instantiation.
